schema.name: ShippingAddress

schema.name: CreatedAt

schema.name: IsVIP
schema.explanation_rich:
**The DAG's punchline.** `IsVIP` is `TRUE` only when a client's *average* invoice value is over $500 **and** they have a recent invoice. `AverageOrderValue` is an `AVERAGEIFS` aggregation over the `Invoices` table; `HasRecentInvoices` is calculated from `LastInvoice` (itself an aggregation). So a single boolean field reaches three tables deep — and every substrate computes it the same way.

**Worked example.** *Brian Lee* has three invoices averaging $657.64 (clears $500) and his most recent is dated 2026-02-20 (within 220 days of today). Both inputs are `TRUE`, so `IsVIP = TRUE` and `Invoices.ClientIsVIP` lights up for every one of his orders on the next read. *Bob*, by contrast, averages $150 — `AverageOrderValue > 500` is `FALSE`, the AND short-circuits, and Bob stays non-VIP no matter how recently he ordered.

data.Name: Caroline
data.StatusDescription: This status is used when the customer or request has been delayed and is not progressing as scheduled.

Clients.summary_rich: The **lifecycle hub.** Every client carries a category, a status, a billing address (which resolves to a state tax rate), and a stack of invoices. `IsVIP` sits at the top of the DAG: it depends on `AverageOrderValue` (aggregation over `Invoices`) AND `HasRecentInvoices` (calculated from `LastInvoice`). Touch one invoice and the VIP flag re-derives.

schema.name: IsApproved
schema.explanation_rich:
**The signature check.** `IsApproved` is just `NOT(ApprovedBy = BLANK())` — a single AppUser FK is what makes an approval real. The interesting part is the *fan-out*: as soon as `ApprovedBy` is set, **four lookup fields** (`ApprovedByContactName`, `ApprovedByEmailAddress`, `ApprovedByPhoneNumber`, `ApprovedByRole`) all materialize the approver's details from `AppUsers`. No join code, no callback, no event — just a re-read.

**Worked example.** *brian-lee-apvd* has `ApprovedBy = ej-ssot-me-admin`. `IsApproved` evaluates to `TRUE` and the four lookups project EJ Alexandra's contact name, ej@ssot.me, (555) 555-1111, and role `Admin` onto the approval row. Compare with *brian-lee-pend*: `ApprovedBy` is blank, so `IsApproved = FALSE` and all four lookups return empty. Same rule, opposite worlds, zero application code.

ClientApprovals.summary_rich: **The v4-distinctive object.** Each row is a signature: a `Client` paired with an `ApprovedBy` `AppUser`. Setting `ApprovedBy` (or clearing it) flips `IsApproved` and materializes the approver's contact name, email, phone, and role through four parallel lookup formulas — the same approval data, projected four ways, with no join code anywhere.

schema.name: StockQuantity
schema.Description: Number of units currently in stock.
schema.explanation_rich:
**The inventory ledger.** v4 doesn't store `StockQuantity` as a raw column — that would lie the moment an adjustment landed somewhere the app didn't watch. Instead, `StockQuantity = SUMIFS` over `InventoryAdjustments.Quantity` filtered by product. The ledger *is* the truth; the field is the rolling total derived from it.

**Worked example.** Product `gadget-200` has one row in `InventoryAdjustments` with `Quantity = 75`, so `StockQuantity = 75`. Insert a restock row of `+200` and `StockQuantity` becomes `275` on the next read; `COGS = StockQuantity * Cost` rebases to `8800`; and `HasBeenOrderedByVIPCustomers` re-evaluates (it needs `StockQuantity > 250`). One ledger row, four cascading recomputes, every substrate sees it.

data.SQLCode:
-- FunctionOverride: calc_orders_item_count
-- Errors in this file are non-fatal (runs without ON_ERROR_STOP).

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.calc_orders_item_count(p_order_id text)
 RETURNS integer
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN ((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM order_line_items WHERE "order" = p_order_id))::integer;
END;
$function$;
data.Name: calc_orders_item_count

data.SQLCode:
-- FunctionOverride: calc_orders_last_payment_date
-- Errors in this file are non-fatal (runs without ON_ERROR_STOP).

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.calc_orders_last_payment_date(p_order_id text)
 RETURNS timestamp with time zone
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN (SELECT MAX(payment_date) FROM payments WHERE "order" = p_order_id);
END;
$function$;
data.Name: calc_orders_last_payment_date

data.SQLCode:
-- FunctionOverride: calc_clients_hast_recent_invoices
-- Errors in this file are non-fatal (runs without ON_ERROR_STOP).
-- Original Airtable formula: =IF({{LastInvoice}} > DATEADD(TODAY(), -180, 'days'), TRUE(), FALSE())
-- Translation failed because DATEADD unit 'days' is unsupported. Reimplement directly.

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.calc_clients_hast_recent_invoices(p_client_id text)
 RETURNS boolean
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN COALESCE(
    (SELECT MAX(order_date) FROM invoices WHERE client = p_client_id) > (NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days'),
    FALSE
  );
END;
$function$;
data.Name: calc_clients_hast_recent_invoices

data.SQLCode:
-- FunctionOverride: calc_orders_payment_count
-- Errors in this file are non-fatal (runs without ON_ERROR_STOP).

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.calc_orders_payment_count(p_order_id text)
 RETURNS integer
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN ((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM payments WHERE "order" = p_order_id))::integer;
END;
$function$;
data.Name: calc_orders_payment_count

__meta__.Description: Project-level metadata that travels with the rulebook: tagline, motif, narrative descriptions, substrate list, signature rows, etc. One row per metadata key. Use ValueType to interpret StringValue vs JsonValue.

schema.name: MetaKey
schema.Description: The metadata key (e.g. 'tagline', 'motif_palette', 'substrates'). Unique within the table.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Identifier for this metadata entry. Mirrors MetaKey so the row is addressable by Name like every other table.

schema.name: ValueType
schema.Description: How to interpret the value columns: 'string' (use StringValue), 'object' (parse JsonValue as JSON object), 'array' (parse JsonValue as JSON array).

schema.name: StringValue
schema.Description: Plain string value. Populated when ValueType == 'string'; null otherwise.

schema.name: JsonValue
schema.Description: JSON-encoded value. Populated when ValueType == 'object' or 'array'; null when ValueType == 'string'.

data.Name: tagline
data.StringValue: The top of the ladder — auth, approval, address normalization, and a full inventory ledger feeding every calculated field.

data.Name: description_rich
data.StringValue: **NakedClaude v4 is the fullest rung of the demo ladder.** Where v1 was one table (Customers), v2 added lifecycle statuses and app users, and v3 layered on invoicing, **v4 closes the loop**: `AppUsers` become real approvers via `ClientApprovals`, `Addresses` normalize through a 50-state taxonomy that feeds order-level `TaxRate`, and `InventoryAdjustments` form a ledger that rolls up into every `Product.StockQuantity`. The whole graph — **ledger → aggregation → lookup chain → calculated boolean → VIP/approval gate** — fires identically in every substrate, with zero application code. This is what 'rulebook as the only source of truth' looks like at full scale.

data.Name: use_cases
data.JsonValue: ["**Approve a pending client.** Open *Brian Lee-PEND*, set `ApprovedBy` to an admin AppUser, and watch `IsApproved` flip TRUE while `ApprovedByContactName`, `ApprovedByEmailAddress`, `ApprovedByPhoneNumber`, and `ApprovedByRole` all materialize from the AppUsers lookup chain.", "**Change a state's tax rate.** Edit `States.TaxRate` for Texas; every Texas address, every client billed there, and every invoice that client placed re-derives its `TaxRate` → `TaxAmount` → `InvoiceTotal` without touching application code.", "**Restock a product.** Insert an `InventoryAdjustments` row for `gadget-200` with `Quantity: 200`; `Products.StockQuantity` rolls up, `COGS` rebases, and `HasBeenOrderedByVIPCustomers` re-evaluates on the next read.", "**Promote a client to VIP.** Add a recent big invoice for *Caroline*; `LastInvoice`, `AverageOrderValue`, `HasRecentInvoices`, and finally `IsVIP` all light up through the DAG — `Invoices.ClientIsVIP` lookups follow on the next read in every substrate.", "**Ask the same question of every substrate.** *\"Which clients are VIP and have an unpaid invoice?\"* — same answer in Postgres, Python, Excel, and OWL, computed from the rulebook, not from glue code."]

data.Name: journal_seed
data.StringValue: Five clients, three approvals on file (two signed, one still pending), eight products tracked against a live inventory ledger, and six invoices threading through a 50-state tax table. The DAG holds — change any leaf and watch the right cells light up everywhere.

data.Name: substrates
data.JsonValue: [{"key": "postgres", "important": true, "chip_label": "Postgres", "witness_sample": "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM vw_clients WHERE \"IsVIP\" = TRUE;"}, {"key": "python", "important": true, "chip_label": "Python", "witness_sample": "sum(1 for c in Clients.all() if c.IsVIP)"}, {"key": "excel", "important": false, "chip_label": "Excel", "witness_sample": "=COUNTIFS(Clients[IsVIP], TRUE)"}, {"key": "owl", "important": false, "chip_label": "OWL", "witness_sample": "Client and (IsVIP value true)"}, {"key": "golang", "important": false, "chip_label": "Go"}]

data.Name: CMCC_Summary
data.StringValue: Airtable export with schema-first type mapping: Schemas, Data, Relationships (FK links), Lookups (INDEX/MATCH), Aggregations (SUMIFS/COUNTIFS/Rollups), and Calculated fields (formulas) in Excel dialect. Field types are determined from Airtable's schema metadata FIRST (no coercion), with intelligent fallback to formula/data analysis only when schema is unavailable.

data.Name: conversion_metadata
data.JsonValue: {"source_base_id": "appeUOAaOIdoqPSx3", "table_count": 15, "tool_version": "2.0.0", "field_type_mapping": "checkbox→boolean, number→number/integer, multipleRecordLinks→relationship, multipleLookupValues→lookup, rollup→aggregation, count→aggregation, formula→calculated", "export_mode": "schema_first_type_mapping", "type_inference": {"enabled": true, "priority": "airtable_metadata (NO COERCION) → formula_analysis → reference_resolution → data_analysis (fallback only)", "airtable_type_mapping": "checkbox→boolean, singleLineText→string, multilineText→string, number→number/integer, datetime→datetime, singleSelect→string, email→string, url→string, phoneNumber→string", "data_coercion_hierarchy": "Only used as fallback when Airtable schema unavailable: datetime → number → integer → boolean → base64 → json → string", "nullable_support": true, "error_value_handling": "#NUM!, #ERROR!, #N/A, #REF!, #DIV/0!, #VALUE!, #NAME? are treated as NULL"}}
